question: Which New York City middle schools had at least 50 specialized high school admissions test offers in every school year from 2015-16 through 2018-19 and are located in Queens District 26? Among those schools, identify the schools named after scientists. How many years apart are the birth years of the earlier-born and later-born scientist namesakes? Please return only the number in the format [Answer].

1.fact:
import io
import pandas as pd
import boto3
from botocore import UNSIGNED
from botocore.config import Config

source = "datagov/2015-2016-shsat-admissions-test-offers-by-sending-school/files/rows.txt"
bucket = "lakeqa-yc4103-datalake"
obj = boto3.client("s3", config=Config(signature_version=UNSIGNED)).get_object(Bucket=bucket, Key=source)
df = pd.read_csv(io.BytesIO(obj["Body"].read()), low_memory=False)

offers = pd.to_numeric(df["Count of Offers"], errors="coerce")
mask = offers >= 50

answer = dict(zip(df.loc[mask, "Feeder School Name"], df.loc[mask, "Feeder School DBN"]))
print(answer)
1.subquestion: Which NYC middle schools had at least 50 SHSAT offers to specialized high schools in school year 2015-16?

4.subquestion: Which NYC middle schools had at least 50 SHSAT offers to specialized high schools in school year 2018-19?

5.fact: According to NYC DOE School Locations data for 2016-17, four schools from the intersection are in Queens District 26: J.H.S. 067 Louis Pasteur (26Q067) in Douglas Manor-Douglaston-Little Neck (NTA QN45), J.H.S. 074 Nathaniel Hawthorne (26Q074) in Oakland Gardens (NTA QN42), M.S. 158 Marie Curie (26Q158) in Bayside-Bayside Hills (NTA QN46), and J.H.S. 216 George J. Ryan (26Q216) in Fresh Meadows-Utopia (NTA QN41). The GEOGRAPHICAL_DISTRICT_CODE for all four is 26.
5.subquestion: Which schools with at least 50 specialized high school admissions test offers in all four school years are located in Queens District 26?

6.fact: Louis Pasteur  (, ; 27 December 1822 – 28 September 1895) was a French chemist, pharmacist, and microbiologist renowned for his discoveries of the principles of vaccination, microbial fermentation, and pasteurization, the last of which was named after him.
6.subquestion: Among the schools in <hop 2 answer> named after a scientist, what was the birth year of the earlier-born scientist namesake?

7.fact: Maria Salomea Skłodowska-Curie (; ; 7 November 1867 – 4 July 1934), better known as Marie Curie ( ; ), was a Polish and naturalised-French physicist and chemist who conducted pioneering research on radioactivity.
7.subquestion: Among the schools in <hop 2 answer> named after a scientist, what was the birth year of the later-born scientist namesake?

Hop result: semantically intersect the yearly school sets from nodes 1-4 = 21 schools.
Node 5: Among the schools from <hop 1 answer>, keep rows where `GEOGRAPHICAL_DISTRICT_CODE` equals 26.
Node 6 uses the school set from hop 2 and identifies the earlier-born scientist namesake's birth year as 1822.

The difference between 1867 and 1822 is 45 years.
